actionLabelHelp: Label of the action (what does it do ?)
activateDebugLogsTooltip: Activate tracing of logs to use the local replay debugger
activateDecomposedTooltip: Activate decomposed format for metadata in your SFDX project (beta feature)
activeUsersCrmWeeklyDescription: Detect CRM users who have been active in the last 7 days
activeUsersExperienceMonthlyDescription: Detect Experience/Portal users active in the last month
advancedDetailsTooltip: Toggle between simple and advanced command details view
aiDocConfigSectionDesc: Configure AI providers and documentation deployment settings. These values are saved in your project's .sfdx-hardis.yml file.
aiProviderSettingsDesc: Select the AI provider, prompts language, and configure provider-specific settings.
aliasCharError: Alias can only contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens
aliasStartError: Alias must start with a letter or underscore
allOrgItemsPackageTooltip: View package-all-org-items.xml - All items in the org
allowFailureHelp: Allow action to fail without marking the deployment as failed
anonymousApexError: 🦙 An error occurred while running the anonymous Apex code.
apexAndFlowErrorsDescription: Monitor recent Apex and Flow runtime errors in your org
apexApiVersionCheckDescription: Identify Apex classes and triggers running on older API versions
apexApiVersionCheckTooltip: Identify custom Apex classes and triggers in a Salesforce org running on older API versions, with details and notifications
apexScriptHelp: Apex script path to run for 'apex' actions
apexTestsConfiguredForPr: Apex test classes configured for this {{prLabel}}.
apexTestsDescription: Run all Apex tests and check code coverage requirements
apexTestsSaved: Apex tests configuration saved for {{prLabel}} #{{prNumber}}. Don't forget to commit and push {{updatedFile}}
apexTestsSavedDraft: Apex tests configuration saved in draft file for the future {{prLabel}}. It will be linked to the {{prLabel}} once created.
apiVersionMismatchTooltip:
Your org is with api version {{orgVersion}} whereas your sfdx project is using api version {{projectVersion}}.
Maybe update sourceApiVersion in your sfdx-project.json? (but be careful if your production org is still using a previous version, you won't be able to deploy in it!)
auditTrailAnalysisDescription: Detect suspicious setup activities in your org's audit trail
autoCloseTooltip: Automatically close this command's panel when it completes successfully
autoFixPullRequestDetectedPrompt: An AI agent has opened {{prLabel}} #{{prNumber}} with suggested fixes for your branch. Do you want to open it?
automationsDocHelp: Generate Automations documentation (Approval Processes, Assignment Rules, AutoResponse Rules, Escalation Rules).
availableWorkspacesHelp:
Each workspace represents a file import/export configuration.
Click on a workspace to select it and see available actions.
Use the Edit and Delete buttons on the right to manage workspaces.
azureDevOpsAuthMethod: How would you like to authenticate to Azure DevOps?
azureDevOpsEnterPAT: Enter your Azure DevOps Personal Access Token with Code (Read & Write) scope
backpromoteDescription: Pull metadata from other User Stories into your sandbox to keep it up to date
backpromoteUserStoryTooltip: Pull metadata changes from other User Stories into your developer sandbox to keep it up to date with the latest branch changes
backupItemsPackageTooltip: View manifest/package-backup-items.xml - Items included in backup
blockedBackgroundModeOnlyHardisOrRegiste: Blocked: In background mode, only sf hardis commands or registered custom/plugin commands are allowed.
blockedCommandNotAllowed:
Blocked: Only 'sf hardis' commands without '&&' are allowed.
{{command}}
blockedOnlySfCommandsNoLogicalOperators: Blocked: Only sf commands without && or || are allowed.\n{{command}}
branchMergeNeededTooltip:
EXPERIMENTAL: There have been new commit(s) into parent branch origin/{{parent}} since you created {{branch}}.
You might need to merge origin/{{parent}} into your current local branch {{branch}}.
After merging, refresh VsCode SFDX-Hardis status panel to discard this warning
Note: Disable disableGitMergeRequiredCheck in settings to skip this check.
branchNotUpToDate: Branch: {{branch}} (not up to date with origin/{{parent}})
checkOrgLimitsTooltip: Checks if limits are reached or soon reached in the default Salesforce org
checkReleaseUpdatesTooltip: Checks if some Release Updates must be verified in the org
cleanProjectTooltip: Select and apply lots of cleaning commands provided by sfdx-hardis
clearLocalAndRemoteSfdxTrackingFiles: Clear local and remote sfdx tracking files
clearLocalAndRemoteTrackingTooltip: Removes all local and remote information about updates you already pulled from org
clickOptionToSelect: Click an option to select, or press Esc to cancel
clickToCreateMergeRequest: Click to create merge request in browser
clickToOpenFolder: Click to open workspace folder in OS explorer
clickToOpenGitRepo: Click to open git repo in browser
clickToOpenGitRepoWithUrl: Click to open git repo in browser - {{url}}
clickToOpenMergeRequest: Click to open merge request in browser
clickToOpenSetup: Click to open Setup in Salesforce
clickToRefreshGit: Git was not ready when SFDX Hardis has been run, please click to refresh
clickToSelectAndAuthenticateDevHub: Click to select and authenticate to a DevHub org
clickToSelectAndAuthenticateOrg: Click to select and authenticate to an org
clickToSelectDefaultOrg: Click to select a default org
clickToUpgradeSfdxPluginTo: Click to upgrade SFDX plugin {{plugin}} to {{version}}
cloneRepositoryTooltip: Clone a repository using VS Code's Git clone command
cloudityServicesDescription: offers professional consulting services to help you maximize SFDX Hardis value and optimize your Salesforce development strategy.
commandNotAllowedOnlyRegistered: This command is not allowed. Only SFDX-Hardis and configured custom/plugin commands can be executed.
commitChangesDescription: Open Metadata Retriever to download metadata changes from your org and commit them to the repository
communityPluginTrustExplanation: This is a community plugin. Using it means you trust its author.
configureDevHubCiAuthTooltip: Assisted configuration to connect to a Dev Hub org during CI
configureOrgCiAuthTooltip: Assisted configuration to connect a protected branch and its related release org during CI
configureOrgMonitoringTooltip: To run only on a repo dedicated to monitoring (start from a blank repo)
configureScratchOrgsPoolTooltip: Define a scratch org pool to have scratch orgs ready to be used for development or CI
confirmForgetNOrgs: This will forget {{count}} orgs (disconnected, deleted, expired or production). Are you sure?
connectedAppsSecurityDescription: Identify unsecured connected apps in your org
connectedToProviderAction: You are connected to {{providerName}}. What would you like to do?
connectGitToSeePrDetails: You need to connect to your Git Server to see pull request details and manage pre-post deployment actions.
connectToOrgTooltip: Connects to a Salesforce org without setting it as defaultusername
couldNotDetectDefaultOrg: Could not detect default org. Please select an org in the UI.
createNewPackageVersionTooltip: Create a new version of a package
createNewScratchOrgTooltip: Create a new scratch org for the current work
createNewSfdxProjectTooltip: Create and initialize a new SFDX project
createScratchOrgResumeTooltip: If during Work:New you had an error, you can resume the scratch org creation
createWorkspaceTooltip: Create a new file import/export workspace
customOrPluginCommandAuthorizationPrompt:
This is a custom or plugin command. How do you want to allow it?
{{command}}
customOrPluginCommandRunInTerminal: This is a custom or plugin command. Run in terminal?
dataStorageStatisticsTooltip: Provides detailed statistics on data storage usage in the org
dataWorkbenchDescription: Import and export data between orgs using SFDMU with full visual configuration support.
dataWorkbenchTooltip: Manage SFDMU data workspaces and run export/import/delete operations with an intuitive interface
deactivateDebugLogsTooltip: Deactivate tracing of logs to use the local replay debugger
debuggerDescription: Debug Salesforce Apex code with integrated log analysis and checkpoint management.
deleteDataFromOrgWithSfdmu: Delete data from org with SFDMU
deleteDataTooltip: Delete data from org using SFDMU config files
deleteScratchOrgsTooltip: Prompts user for scratch orgs to mark for deletion
depGitDeltaExplanation: sfdx-git-delta helps to generate package.xml/diff based on your git changes.
depGitDownloadLink: Download and install Git (with Git Bash)
depGitExplanation: Git is the VCS (Version Control System) used to handle your Salesforce project sources. It also provides Git Bash for Windows.
depGitMergeDriverExplanation: sf-git-merge-driver is a Git merge driver for Salesforce metadata files to reduce merge conflicts.
depGitMissingMessage: Git is not installed or not found in PATH
deployDocumentationSectionDesc: Deploy your generated documentation to Cloudflare Pages, Confluence, or as a Salesforce Static Resource.
deploymentActionMissingRequiredFields: Please fill in all required fields before saving.
deploymentActionSaved: Deployment action saved for {{prLabel}} #{{prNumber}}. Don't forget to commit and push {{updatedFile}}
deploymentActionSavedDraft: Deployment action saved in draft file for the future {{prLabel}}. It will be linked to the {{prLabel}} once created.
deploymentsAnalysisDescription: Analyze metadata deployments and validations in your org
deployToCloudflareDesc: Build HTML docs and publish them to Cloudflare Pages for public or internal access.
deployToConfluenceDesc: Build HTML docs and publish them to a Confluence space. Requires a Confluence API token configured.
deployToSalesforceDesc: Build HTML docs and upload as a Static Resource with a VisualForce Page and Custom Tab. Note: Only works with small projects (5MB max for static resources).
depNodeDownloadLink: Download and install Node.js (use Windows Installer)
depNodeExplanation: Node.js is required to run Salesforce CLI and its plugins.
depNodeMissingMessage: Node.js is not installed or not found in PATH (required minimum version: {{version}})
depNodeOutdatedMessage:
Installed NodeJS major version {{major}} is older than the recommended one {{minMajor}}.
It is recommended to install NodeJS {{minMajor}}, then restart VsCode.
depSfCliExplanation: The modern Salesforce CLI (sf) is required to run Salesforce commands used by the extension.
depSfCliLegacyMessage: Legacy sfdx-cli detected. Please uninstall it using `npm uninstall sfdx-cli -g` then upgrade to @salesforce/cli.
depSfCliNonNpmMessage:
Non-npm installation detected at {{path}} (bad installation using Salesforce website executable installer).
Please uninstall Salesforce CLI in "Windows -> Uninstall program" (or the equivalent on Mac), then re-install using sfdx-hardis Wizard (NPM-based).
depSfCliOutdatedMessage: Your sf CLI version {{version}} differs from recommended {{recommended}}
depSfdmuExplanation: SFDMU (Salesforce Data Move Utility) is used for data import/export workflows integrated in the extension.
depSfdxHardisExplanation: sfdx-hardis is the main plugin this extension integrates with. Keep it up to date for features and bugfixes.
depSfdxHardisOldMessage: Your sfdx-hardis plugin version ({{version}}) is older than the recommended {{minimal}}
depSfPackagingExplanation: @salesforce/plugin-packaging provides packaging commands used for package creation and versioning.
depSfPluginOutdatedMessage: A newer version ({{latest}}) of {{plugin}} is available
descriptionHelp:
Optional description to explain what this workspace is used for.
This helps team members understand the purpose and scope of this file export configuration.
descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this workspace is used for...
detectDuplicateSfdxFilesTooltip: Detects if duplicate files are within in your SFDX project
detectDuplicateValuesInMetadataFiles: Detect duplicate values in metadata files
detectDuplicateValuesTooltip: Detects if duplicate values for given keys are within in your SFDX metadata files
detectLegacyApiTooltip: Detects if deprecated APIs are used in a production org
devOpsPipelineDescription: Visualize and manage your CI/CD pipeline with automated deployments and environment management.
devOpsPipelineTooltip: Open the DevOps Pipeline panel to visualize and manage your CI/CD pipeline.
disableGitBashCheckHint: If you do not want to see this message anymore, set VsCode setting vsCodeSfdxHardis.disableGitBashCheck to true, or click on Dont ask again.
displayLabelHelp:
A human-readable name for this workspace.
This will be displayed in the workspace list and helps identify the purpose of this configuration.
displayLiveLogsTooltip: Display apex logs in console while they are generated
docLanguageHelp: Override the language used for AI-generated documentation. Applies PROMPTS_LANGUAGE and SFDX_HARDIS_LANG env vars. Defaults to VS Code language setting.
documentationConfigSubtitle: Configure AI providers, prompt templates, and deployment options
documentationDescription: Generate comprehensive project documentation including flows, objects, and deployment guides.
documentationWorkbenchDescription: Generate, deploy, and manage project documentation from one place.
documentationWorkbenchSubtitle: Generate, deploy and manage your Salesforce project documentation
documentationWorkbenchTooltip: Open the Documentation Workbench to generate, deploy, and manage project documentation
draftActionsFileAssociated: Draft deployment actions file has been found and associated to {{prLabel}} #{{prNumber}}. Don't forget to commit & push 😇
duplicateCommandWarning: No need to click multiple times on a menu, just be patient.
duplicateConfigFilesDetected: Both .sfdx-hardis.yml (root) and config/.sfdx-hardis.yml exist. Only one global config file is allowed. Do you want to merge config/.sfdx-hardis.yml into .sfdx-hardis.yml (root) and delete config/.sfdx-hardis.yml?
duplicateConfigFilesMergeSuccess: config/.sfdx-hardis.yml has been merged into .sfdx-hardis.yml and deleted.
dwbAllOrNoneHelp: If enabled, SFDMU will rollback the batch on errors (allOrNone behaviour).
dwbAlwaysBulkHelp: Force Bulk API for both query and DML operations of this object.
dwbAlwaysBulkToggle: Always use Bulk API (query and DML)
dwbAlwaysBulkUpdateHelp: Force Bulk API for DML operations even when REST override is not active.
dwbAlwaysBulkUpdateToggle: Always use Bulk API for DML updates
dwbAlwaysRestHelp: Force REST API for both query and DML operations of this object.
dwbAlwaysRestToggle: Always use REST API (query and DML)
dwbAvailableWorkspacesHelp:
Each workspace represents a SFDMU export.json configuration.
Click on a workspace to view actions and details.
dwbBadgeAllowTruncation: Allow truncation of string field values
dwbBadgeQueryBulkThreshold: Records threshold for Bulk API queries
dwbBadgeRestUpdates: Force REST API for all DML updates
dwbBadgeTargetCsv: Write target data to CSV files
dwbBulkApiBatchSizeHelp: Maximum number of records per batch when using Bulk API V1. Leave blank to use SFDMU default (9500).
dwbCreateWorkspaceTooltip: Create a new data import/export workspace
dwbDeleteByHierarchyHelp: Delete records along with their related hierarchy. Used with Delete operation.
dwbDeleteDataTooltip: Delete data in Salesforce using this workspace
dwbDeleteFromSourceHelp: Delete records from the source org instead of the target org.
dwbDeleteOldDataHelp: If enabled, records not present in the CSV/data set will be deleted in target org.
dwbDeleteOldDataToggle: Delete records missing from the dataset
dwbDeleteQueryHelp: Optional SOQL query to select which old records to delete from the target. If omitted, the main query is used.
dwbDesc: Manage SFDMU data workspaces and run export/import/delete operations
dwbDescriptionFieldHelp: Optional description to explain the purpose of this SFDMU configuration.
dwbDescriptionFieldPlaceholder: Describe what this workspace is used for...
dwbDisplayLabelHelp:
A human-readable name for this workspace.
Displayed in the list to identify the SFDMU project.
dwbEditGlobalSettingsTooltip: Edit SFDMU global settings for this workspace
dwbEditPropertiesTooltip: Edit workspace name, label and description
dwbExcludedFieldsHelp: Field names to exclude from the migration. These fields are omitted from the SOQL query entirely.
dwbExcludedFromUpdateFieldsHelp: Field names that are retrieved but NOT updated in the target org. Useful when fields are needed for transformation but should not be modified.
dwbExcludeHelp: Temporarily disable this object without removing its configuration.
dwbExportDataTooltip: Export data from Salesforce using this workspace
dwbExternalIdHelp: External ID field used for upsert or relationship resolution. Can be a single external ID field (ex: 'ExternalId') or a composite field (Ex: 'Name;FirstName;BirthDate' or 'Article__r.Name;Language__r.Name')
dwbFieldMappingHelp: Enable custom field mapping between source and target fields. Define mappings in the fieldMapping array or via CSV.
dwbGlobalSettingsDesc: Configure script-level settings for this workspace's export.json
dwbGsAllOrNone: All or None (global rollback on errors)
dwbGsAllOrNoneHelp: Global setting: rollback the batch on any error. Can be overridden per object.
dwbGsAllOrNoneToggle: All or None (global rollback on errors)
dwbGsAllowTruncationHelp: Allow truncation of string field values that exceed the field length limit instead of failing.
dwbGsAlwaysRestUpdateHelp: Force all update operations to use REST API instead of Bulk API.
dwbGsAlwaysRestUpdateToggle: Always use REST API for updates
dwbGsApiVersionHelp: Salesforce API version to use (e.g. 62.0). Leave blank for default.
dwbGsBinaryDataCacheHelp: Caching strategy for binary data (Attachments, ContentVersions). InMemory, FileCache, or CleanFileCache.
dwbGsBulkApiVersionHelp: Version of Bulk API to use: 1.0 or 2.0.
dwbGsBulkBatchSizeHelp: Default batch size for Bulk API v1 operations. Default: 9500.
dwbGsBulkThresholdHelp: Number of records above which SFDMU switches from REST to Bulk API. Default: 200.
dwbGsBulkV1BatchSizeHelp: Default batch size for Bulk API v1 operations. Default: 9500.
dwbGsConcurrencyModeHelp: Bulk API concurrency mode: Parallel (default) or Serial. Serial is slower but avoids lock contention.
dwbGsCreateCsvHelp: Write target data results to CSV files after migration.
dwbGsCreateTargetCsvHelp: Write target data results to CSV files after migration.
dwbGsCsvAlwaysQuotedHelp: Wrap all CSV field values in quotes, regardless of content.
dwbGsCsvDelimiterHelp: Character used as field delimiter in CSV files. Default is comma.
dwbGsCsvEuropeanDate: Use European Date Format in CSV (dd/MM/yyyy)
dwbGsCsvEuropeanDateHelp: Parse and write dates in European format (dd/MM/yyyy) instead of US format (MM/dd/yyyy).
dwbGsCsvExcludeIds: Exclude record IDs from CSV exports
dwbGsCsvExcludeIdsHelp: Omit Salesforce record IDs from exported CSV files.
dwbGsCsvImportAsIsHelp: Import CSV records directly without any field mapping or transformation.
dwbGsCsvInsertNullsHelp: When enabled, #N/A values in CSV are treated as NULL and clear the target field.
dwbGsCsvSeparatedFiles: Use separated CSV files per object
dwbGsCsvSeparatedFilesHelp: Store CSV files in separate subfolders for each SObject.
dwbGsCsvUppercaseHeaders: Write Uppercase Column Headers in CSV
dwbGsCsvUppercaseHeadersHelp: Convert CSV column headers to uppercase when writing.
dwbGsCsvUppercaseHelp: Write CSV column headers in uppercase letters.
dwbGsCsvUtf8Bom: Use UTF-8 BOM in CSV files
dwbGsCsvUtf8BomHelp: Write UTF-8 Byte Order Mark at the beginning of CSV files. Helps Excel detect encoding.
dwbGsCsvUtf8BomToggle: Use UTF-8 BOM in CSV Files
dwbGsCsvValidateOnlyHelp: Only validate the CSV files without performing any data operations.
dwbGsExcludedObjectsHelp: Comma-separated list of SObject names to globally exclude from the migration. Example: Task, Event
dwbGsExcludeIdsCsvHelp: Do not include Salesforce Id columns in exported CSV files.
dwbGsExcludeIdsCsvToggle: Exclude Id Fields from CSV Files
dwbGsImportCsvAsIsHelp: Import CSV files without any preprocessing or column remapping.
dwbGsKeepOrderHelp: Process objects in the exact order they appear in the config. By default, SFDMU optimizes execution order based on dependencies.
dwbGsParallelBinaryDownloadsHelp: Maximum number of parallel binary file (Attachment/ContentVersion) downloads.
dwbGsParallelBinaryHelp: Max number of parallel binary data (Attachment, ContentVersion) downloads. Default: 1.
dwbGsParallelBulkHelp: Max number of concurrent Bulk API jobs. Default: 1.
dwbGsParallelBulkJobsHelp: Maximum number of parallel Bulk API jobs to run simultaneously.
dwbGsParallelRestHelp: Max number of concurrent REST API jobs. Default: 1.
dwbGsParallelRestJobsHelp: Maximum number of parallel REST API jobs to run simultaneously.
dwbGsPollingIntervalHelp: Interval in milliseconds to poll for Bulk API job status. Default: 5000.
dwbGsPollingQueryTimeoutHelp: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a Bulk API query to complete.
dwbGsPromptCsvIssues: Prompt on issues in CSV files
dwbGsPromptCsvIssuesHelp: Show a confirmation prompt when issues are detected in CSV files.
dwbGsPromptMissingParentsHelp: Show a confirmation prompt when parent objects referenced by lookups are not in the export.json.
dwbGsProxyUrlHelp: HTTP proxy URL for all outbound connections. Example: http://proxy.example.com:8080
dwbGsQueryBulkThresholdHelp: Number of records above which SFDMU uses Bulk API for queries. Default: 30000.
dwbGsRestBatchSizeHelp: Default batch size for REST API operations. Default: 9500.
dwbGsRestUpdates: Always use REST API for DML updates (global)
dwbGsRestUpdatesHelp: Force REST API for all DML update operations regardless of object configuration.
dwbGsSeparatedCsvHelp: Store object data in separate CSV files per object instead of a single file.
dwbGsSimulationHelp: Run in simulation mode: queries run but no records are actually created, updated, or deleted.
dwbGsSimulationModeHelp: Run the migration without actually performing any DML operations. Useful for testing and validation.
dwbGsValidateCsvHelp: Validate CSV files and report issues without importing any data.
dwbHardDeleteHelp: Permanently delete records bypassing the Salesforce recycle bin. Requires Bulk API Hard Delete permission.
dwbImportDataTooltip: Import data to Salesforce using this workspace
dwbMasterHelp: Checked (default): no relation filter is applied. Unchecked: records are filtered by relations to results from queries on objects where master=true.
dwbNoExportedFiles: No exported files yet. Run an export to generate CSV files.
dwbNoLogFiles: No log files yet. Run an export or import to generate logs.
dwbNoObjectsConfigured: No objects configured yet. Click "Add Object" to add your first object.
dwbNoWorkspacesFoundDesc: Create your first data workspace to get started.
dwbObjBadgeAllOrNone: Rollback all updates if they are not all successful
dwbObjBadgeCsvSource: Use CSV file as source instead of org
dwbObjBadgeDeleteOldData: Records not present in the CSV/data set will be deleted in target org.
dwbObjBadgeDeleteSource: Delete from source org instead of target
dwbObjBadgeExcluded: This object is excluded from migration
dwbObjBadgeHardDelete: Records are permanently deleted, bypassing recycle bin
dwbObjBadgeMasterFiltered: Records are filtered using results from master queries
dwbObjBadgeMockData: Selected fields values will be replaced by random values during upload or download
dwbObjBadgeQueryAllTarget: Query all records from target ignoring WHERE/LIMIT
dwbObjectHelp: Object name inferred from SOQL query.
dwbOpenFolderTooltip: Open workspace folder in OS explorer
dwbParallelBulkJobsHelp: Maximum number of Bulk API jobs running in parallel for this object. Default: 1.
dwbParallelRestJobsHelp: Number of REST API jobs that can run in parallel. Default: 1.
dwbQueryAllTargetHelp: When enabled, all records from the target org are queried, ignoring any WHERE, LIMIT or OFFSET clauses. Useful for complete synchronization.
dwbQueryAllTargetToggle: Query all records from target (ignore WHERE/LIMIT)
dwbRespectOrderDeleteHelp: Enables order-preserving delete mode. Forces REST with one record per request. Can be slow and consume more API limits.
dwbRespectOrderDeleteToggle: Respect ORDER BY on delete (one record per request)
dwbRespectOrderHelp: Enables order-preserving delete mode. Forces REST with one record per request. Can be slow and consume more API limits.
dwbRespectOrderToggle: Respect ORDER BY on delete (one record per request)
dwbRestApiBatchSizeHelp: Maximum number of records per batch when using REST API. Useful for large binary objects like Attachments.
dwbSelectWorkspaceDesc: Click on a workspace from the list to see available actions and configuration details.
dwbSkipComparisonHelp: When enabled, SFDMU skips comparing source and target records, updating all records regardless of whether data matches.
dwbSkipExistingHelp: When enabled, updates are skipped for records that already exist in the target. Only new records are inserted.
dwbSoqlQueryHelp: SOQL query defining records for this object. Ensure required External Id is included when using Upsert.
dwbSourceRecordsFilterHelp: Filter expression applied to source records after retrieval and before DML processing. Example: Name LIKE 'ACC_%'
dwbTargetRecordsFilterHelp: AlaSQL filter expression applied to target records before update. Example: CreatedDate > LAST_N_DAYS:30
dwbUpdateWithMockDataHelp: Enable mock data generation for fields defined below.
dwbUpdateWithMockHelp: Enable mock data generation for fields defined below.
dwbUseCsvSourceHelp: Use a CSV file as the data source instead of querying the source org for this specific object.
dwbUseCsvSourceToggle: Use CSV file as data source for this object
dwbUseQueryAllHelp: If enabled, SFDMU uses QueryAll to include archived and soft-deleted records.
dwbUseQueryAllToggle: Include archived and deleted records in query results (QueryAll)
dwbValuesMappingHelp: Enable value mapping for this object via ValueMapping.csv file.
dwbWorkspaceNameHelp:
The folder name for this workspace.
This will create a folder in scripts/data/ directory.
enterColorPrompt: Please enter a color code for {{org}} (type "color picker" in google to get one)
enterJiraPat: Enter your JIRA Personal Access Token
errorGettingPrInfo: Error getting {{prLabel}} info. Please check the logs for details.
executeWithCustomUsernameHelp: Optional custom username to use to run the action. If you input user@company.com, user@company.com.uat and user.company.com.preprod will be found in related orgs.
executionContextsHelp: Contexts when the action must be run
exportDataFromOrgWithSfdmu: Export data from org with SFDMU
exportDataTooltip: Export data from org to project files using SFDMU
exportFilesTooltip: Export files from Salesforce using this workspace configuration
extractApiVersionsTooltip: Browse all project files and summarize API versions of elements
extractCustomLabelTranslationsTooltip: Extract selected custom labels, or of a given Lightning Web Component (LWC), from all language translation files
extractProfilesPersonasTooltip: Extract Salesforce profiles, personas, and related metadata into structured CSV/XLSX deliverables, covering users, record types, apps, permissions, fields, and permission sets
extractPullRequestsTooltip: Extract Pull Requests and associated ticketing system references in a CSV / Excel file
failedConnectGitProvider: Failed to connect to Git provider. Please check the logs for details.
failedConnectToProvider: Failed to connect to {{providerName}}. Please check the logs for details.
failedToOpenOrgCliAndUrl: Failed to open org via CLI and URL: {{error}}
failedToRetrieveMetadataDueToConflicts: Failed to retrieve metadata due to source conflicts.
failedToRunVsCodeCommand: Failed to run VS Code command: {{command}}
fileNameFormatHelp:
How to name the exported files:
- title: Use the original file title
- title_id: Title + Salesforce ID
- id_title: Salesforce ID + Title
- id: Only the Salesforce ID
filesWorkbenchDesc: Manage file import/export workspaces and run file operations
filesWorkbenchDescription: Import and export files and attachments between Salesforce orgs with ease.
filesWorkbenchTooltip: Manage file import/export workspaces and run file operations with an intuitive interface
fileTypesHelp:
Filter which file types to include in the export/import.
'all' = all file types
'PDF' = only PDF files
Or specify comma-separated extensions like 'PDF,DOC,DOCX'
fileTypesInputHelp: Comma-separated list of file extensions to include (e.g. pdf,docx). Leave empty to include all file types.
filterByTypeOrMember: Filter by type or member name...
flowsDocHelp: Generate Flows, Process Builders and Workflows documentation.
flowsDocumentationPdfTooltip: Generates Visual Documentation for all Flows + PDF files
flowsDocumentationTooltip: Generates Visual Documentation for all Flows
folderNameFieldHelp:
The field from your SOQL query to use as folder names.
Example: If you use 'Name', files will be organized in folders named after the record's Name field.
If you want to import files in another orgs, you probably should use an ExternalID field.
formatsAndHistoryHelp: Control the output format and change tracking.
freezeUsersTooltip: Freeze all users of an org to perform a safe deployment
generateBypassCustomPermissions: Generate bypass custom permissions and permission sets
generateBypassTooltip: Generates bypass custom permissions and permission sets for specified sObjects and automations (Flows, Triggers, and Validation Rules)
generateDocumentationSectionDesc: Generate Markdown documentation for your Salesforce project. Includes Flows, Apex, Objects, Permissions, and more.
generateExcelHelp: Also generate summary in Excel alongside Markdown.
generateMarkdownHelp: Always generated (project docs in Markdown format).
generateNewPasswordTooltip: Generates a new password for your current scratch org user
generateOrgFullPackageXmlTooltip: Generate full package.xml from any org
generatePackageXmlGitDeltaTooltip: Generate delta package.xml between two git commits
generatePdfHelp: Also generate PDF files alongside Markdown.
gettingStartedDescription: New to SFDX Hardis? Start by connecting to a Salesforce org, then explore our comprehensive documentation to understand your Salesforce environment and streamline your development workflow.
gettingStartedSubtitle: Quick start guide for new users
gitBashRecommendedAsDefaultTerminal: It is recommended to use Git Bash as default terminal shell (do it in the opened dialog at the top of the screen).
giteaEnterPAT: Enter your Gitea PAT (Personal Access Token)
gitlabEnterPAT: Enter your Gitlab PAT (Personal Access Token)
gitLoginErrorsTooltip: Use this command in case you have git login errors
gitMergeDriverDisablingBeforeUpgrade: Disabling Salesforce Git Merge Driver before upgrade...
gitMergeDriverReenablingAfterUpgrade: Re-enabling Salesforce Git Merge Driver after upgrade...
gitNotInstalled: 🦙 You need Git installed on your computer. Please download and install it (select GIT BASH in options), then restart VsCode
gitNotReadyClickToRefresh: Git not ready: click to refresh
gitProviderAuthError: Error during Git provider authentication. Please check the logs for details.
hardisCommandNotAllowedInBackground: Error: SFDX-Hardis command not allowed in background mode.
howUserInputIsHandled: How user input is handled in the extension.
importDataTooltip: Import data into org from project files
importDataToOrgWithSfdmu: Import data to org with SFDMU
importFilesTooltip: Import files to Salesforce using this workspace configuration
inactiveMetadatasTooltip: Check if flows or validation rules are inactive, so should be deleted
includeSectionHelp: Select which documentation parts to generate.

Minimum file size in kilobytes to include in export/import.
Files smaller than this size will be skipped.
Set to 0 to include all files regardless of size.
minFileSizeInputHelp: Minimum file size in bytes to include (0 = no minimum).

Start to work, it will:
- Create a new git branch where it is needed to 
- Allow to select or create a Salesforce org to work in (sandbox or scratch)

orgMonitoringNotInstalledDescription: Install org monitoring to start tracking your Salesforce org's health, security, and performance.

otherSettings: Other settings related to the extension.
overridePromptTemplatesDesc: Copy default templates into your project so you can customize them. Existing local templates are preserved.

overridePromptTemplatesTooltip: Override and customize documentation prompt templates for your project
overwriteExistingFilesHelp:
If checked, replaces existing local files with the same name.
If unchecked, skips downloading files that already exist locally.
overwriteParentRecordsHelp:
If checked, will re-export files for records that already have a local folder.
If unchecked, skips records that already have been processed.
packagesDeploymentsInfo: Packages flagged Deployments will be installed during sfdx-hardis deployment workflows.
packagesScratchOrgInfo: Packages flagged Scratch Org will be installed when creating Scratch Orgs for development or validation purposes.
packagingTooltip: Second generation packages, unlocked or managed
pasteSalesforceValidationLink: Please paste your Salesforce validation link here
pipelineDevOpsComparison: See how sfdx-hardis can help you automate and secure your Salesforce deployments, and compare with other DevOps solutions.
pipelineSettingsForBranch: Pipeline Settings for major git branch
pkgAllOrgDescription: All items in the org including non-backed up items

poolTooltip: View content of scratch org pool
prClickToManageActions: #{{num}} - {{title}}. Click to see related tickets and manage pre-post deployment actions.
pressEnterToValidate: Press Enter to validate, Esc to cancel
prNotCreatedYet: {{prLabel}} not created yet. Click to manage pre-post deployment actions.
productionOrgWarning: 🦙 Your default org is a PRODUCTION org, be careful what you do 😲
profilesDocHelp: Generate Profiles, Permission Sets, Permission Set Groups and Roles documentation.
projectDocumentationPdfTooltip: Generates markdown pages with SF Project content: List of metadatas, installed packages... + PDF files
projectDocumentationTooltip: Generates markdown pages with SF Project content: List of metadatas, installed packages...
projectDocumentationWithHistoryTooltip: Generates markdown pages with SF Project content: List of metadatas, installed packages..., with Flow Diff History
promptTemplatesSectionDesc: Override and customize AI prompt templates used for documentation generation. These templates control how LLMs describe Flows, Objects, Apex classes, etc.
pullFromSfOrgToLocalFiles: Pull from SF Org to local files
pullFromSfOrgToLocalFilesTooltip:
Once you have made your configuration in your org Setup, click here to download your updates.
Then, you can commit the updates you want to publish (use VsCode Git extension)
Then you can run command "Save / Publish User Story"
Note: if you don't see all your updates, you can manually retrieve it using VsCode Extension "Org Browser"(Salesforce logo)
purgeApexLogsTooltip: Purge all apex logs of default org
purgeObsoleteFlowsTooltip: Purge all flows with status Obsolete in your org, so you are not bothered by the 50 versions limits
purgeProfilePermissions: Purge Permission Sets attributes from Profiles
purgeProfilePermissionsTooltip: Remove or mute Permission Sets attributes from selected Salesforce Profile metadata files and redeploy the cleaned profiles to the target org. Useful after a migration from Profile-based to PS-based permission management
pushFromLocalFilesToSalesforceOrg: Push from local files to Salesforce org
pushToSalesforceOrgTooltip: Propagates your local updates within Vs Code into your remote Salesforce scratch org
readOnlyBranchModeApexTests: Read-only in branch mode. Open a single {{prLabel}} to edit.
reconfigureAuthTooltip: Reconfigure authentication to the monitored org
releaseUpdatesDescription: Check for Salesforce release updates that need verification
removeInvalidEmailsTooltip: Removes the .invalid of all users emails in a sandbox
remoteBranchUpdatedPrompt: New commits are available on origin/{{branch}} for your current {{prLabel}}. Pull them now?
removeTrackingFilesTooltip: Removes all local information about updates you already pulled from org
resetSelectedListOfItemsToMerge: Reset selected list of items to merge
resetSelectedListTooltip:
You already pushed a commit but you selected updates that you do not want to deploy ?
In that case, click here and you'll be able to select again what you want to commit.
After creating new commits, click on "Save / Publish User Story"
retrieveAllDxSourcesFromOrg: Retrieve ALL DX sources from an org
retrieveAllDxSourcesTooltip: Retrieve locally all the metadatas of a remote salesforce org, in DX project format
retrieveAllMetadataSourcesFromOrg: Retrieve ALL Metadata sources from an org
retrieveAllMetadataSourcesTooltip: Retrieve locally all the metadatas of a remote salesforce org, in metadata format
retrieveAnalyticsSourcesTooltip: Allows user to select a list of metadata types and process the retrieve from an org
retrieveApexForDebugTooltip: Retrieve sources from your org so you can use the replay debugger
retrieveDxSourcesFromOrg: Retrieve DX sources from an org (package.xml)
retrieveDxSourcesFromOrgTooltip: Retrieve DX sources from any org using a package.xml file
retrievePackagesConfigFromOrgTooltip: Retrieve package configuration from an org and propose to update project sfdx-hardis configuration
runAnonymousApexDescription: Execute anonymous Apex code directly in your Salesforce org for testing and debugging, like in Developer Console
runAnonymousApexTooltip: Execute anonymous apex code like in Developer Console
runApexTestsTooltip: Runs all apex tests on the selected org. Will trigger error if minimum apex code coverage is not reached
runDebuggerTooltip: Run debugger on an apex log file
runDuplicateCommandAnyway: No, you are wrong, let me run it again.
runLocalHtmlDocPagesDesc: Starts a local web server at http://127.0.0.1:8000/ to preview your documentation. Python must be installed.
runLocalHtmlDocTooltip: Run Documentation local web server, then open http://127.0.0.1:8000/ . You need Python on your computer
runLocallySectionDesc: Start a local MkDocs server to preview the documentation in your browser (requires Python).
runOnlyOnceByOrgHelp: Run this action only once per target org (useful for idempotent post-deploy tasks)
salesforceExtensionPackError:
🦙 Salesforce Extension pack command error. If it is installed, just wait for it to be initialized 🤗
Detail: {{detail}}
sandboxRefreshAfterTooltip: Restore info after a sandbox refresh (Connected Apps, Custom Settings...)
sandboxRefreshBeforeTooltip: Store info that will be needed after a sandbox refresh (Connected Apps, Custom Settings...)
savePublishDescription: Save your work and publish changes to the remote repository
savePublishUserStoryTooltip:
Once you performed your commit(s), click here to prepare your Pull Request. It will:
- Automatically update package.xml and destructiveChanges.xml
- Clean metadatas before publishing them (for example remove flow positions or remove object & field access from Profiles)
scheduleBatchClassNameHelp: Name of the Apex class implementing Schedulable for schedule-batch actions
scheduleBatchCronExpressionExample: Examples: '0 0 2 * * ?' means every day at 2 AM, '0 0 8 ? * MON' means every Monday at 8 AM
scheduleBatchCronExpressionHelp: Salesforce cron expression for schedule-batch actions
scheduleBatchJobNameHelp: Optional scheduled job name for schedule-batch actions. Defaults to <className>_Schedule
searchingOrgsForInstanceUrl: Searching orgs for a matching instance URL...
sectionApexTestsSecurityDescription: Run Apex tests and monitor security indicators
sectionLicensesPackagesDescription: Track license usage and installed packages
sectionOrgActivityDescription: Monitor metadata backup, errors, audit trail and deployments
sectionOrgInfoDescription: Monitor release updates and org instance information
sectionOrgLimitsDescription: Monitor if org limits are close to being reached
sectionTechnicalDebtDescription: Detect unused, inactive or undocumented metadata
sectionUserActivityDescription: Monitor active and inactive users in your org
securityHealthCheckDescription: Extract score and indicators of org Security health check
securityHealthCheckTooltip: Extract score and indicators of org Security health check
selectAndApplyCleaningCommands: Select and apply lots of cleaning commands provided by sfdx-hardis
selectAndRetrieveSfdxSourcesFromOrg: Select and retrieve sfdx sources from org
selectAndRetrieveSourcesTooltip: Allows user to select a list of metadata types and process the retrieve from an org
selectApexTestClasses: Select Apex test classes to run.
selectWorkspaceDesc: Click on a workspace from the list to see available actions and configuration details.
sfCliUninstallFirstTooltip: First, you need to uninstall Salesforce DX / Salesforce CLI from Windows -> Programs -> Uninstall program (or equivalent on MAC)
sfdmuProjectHelp: Path to SFDMU project for 'data' actions
sfdxDeprecatedTooltip: sfdx is deprecated: Install the latest Salesforce CLI, please click to make the upgrade
sfdxHardisPluginOutdated:
Your sfdx-hardis plugin version ({{version}}) is outdated. Please upgrade to latest version to benefit from new features.
Run: sf plugins:install sfdx-hardis@{{versionToInstall}}
sfdxHardisPreReleaseAlphaMessage:
You are using VsCode sfdx-hardis pre-release version. Please install alpha version of sfdx-hardis plugin to benefit from new features.
Run: sf plugins:install sfdx-hardis@alpha
sfdxHardisPreReleaseBetaMessage:
You are using VsCode sfdx-hardis pre-release version. Please install beta version of sfdx-hardis plugin to benefit from new features.
Run: sf plugins:install sfdx-hardis@beta
sfdxPluginLatestInstalled: Latest version of SFDX plugin {{plugin}} is installed
singleFlowVisualGitDiffTooltip: Generates Visual Documentation of the differences between versions of a Flow
skipIfDeploymentErrorHelp: Do not run the action if there is a deployment error
skipItemsPackageTooltip: View manifest/package-skip-items.xml - Items ignored during backup
somePluginsNotUpToDate: � Some plugins are not up to date, please click to upgrade, then wait for the process to be completed before performing actions
soqlQueryHelp:
The SOQL query to fetch parent records for file operations.
Example: SELECT Id,Name FROM Opportunity WHERE Name LIKE 'Important%'
Files attached to these records will be exported/imported.
soqlQueryInputHelp: The SOQL query to fetch parent records for file operations. Example: SELECT Id, Name FROM Opportunity
successfullyDeletedNLocalFiles: Successfully deleted {{count}} local file(s) corresponding to deleted metadata.
suspiciousAuditTrailTooltip: Detect setup actions in major orgs that are identified as Suspicious
thisWillForgetNOrgs: This will forget {{count}} orgs (disconnected, deleted, expired or production). Are you sure?
unableToFindTicketingConnection: Unable to find active ticketing provider connection.
unableToOpenOrg: Unable to open org: no target-org or instance URL provided.
underusedPermissionSetsDescription: Find permission sets with few or no active assignments
underusedPermissionSetsTooltip: Identify permission sets that have very few or no active assignments in the org, helping you optimize your access management configuration
unfreezeUsersTooltip: Unfreeze all users of an org after a safe deployment
unsecuredConnectedAppsTooltip: List all Connected Apps that are unsecured (not installed or not Admin user pre-approved)
unusedApexClassesDescription: Find Apex classes that haven't been used recently
unusedApexClassesTooltip: List all async Apex classes (Batch,Queueable,Schedulable) that has not been called for more than 365 days, and could probably be deleted
unusedConnectedAppsDescription: Find connected apps that haven't been used recently
unusedConnectedAppsTooltip: List all Connected Apps that have not been used for more than 90 days, and could probably be deleted
unusedLicensesDescription: Detect unused permission set licenses that can be reclaimed
unusedMetadataDescription: Find unused custom labels and permissions
unusedMetadatasDescription: Identify unused metadata components in your org to clean up and reduce complexity.
unusedMetadatasTooltip: Check if elements (custom labels and custom permissions) are not used in the project
unusedPsLicensesTooltip: Detects if there are unused permission set licenses in the org, and offers to delete them
unusedUsersCrm6MonthsDescription: Detect CRM users who have not logged in for 6 months
unusedUsersDescription: Find active users who haven't logged in recently
unusedUsersExperience6MonthsDescription: Detect Experience/Portal users who have not logged in for 6 months
unusedUsersTooltip: Identify active users who haven't logged in recently to the org
uploadHtmlDocToConfluenceTooltip: Generates HTML Doc and publishes it to a Confluence space. Requires a Confluence API token configured
uploadHtmlDocToSalesforceTooltip: Generates HTML Doc and Uploads it to Salesforce as a static resource with a VfPage & a CustomTab. You need Python on your computer
usernameTooltip:
Username on your remote Salesforce org: {{username}}
Click to Open User Settings in Salesforce
usingLocallyDevelopedPlugin: You are using locally developed version of {{plugin}}
viewScratchOrgPool: View content of scratch org pool

vsCodeNotInitialized:
VsCode SFDX-Hardis is not initialized yet, please wait a few seconds before running this command again.
If the problem persists, update VsCode setting vsCodeSfdxHardis.userInputCommandLineIfLWC to 'terminal'
vscodeSfdxHardisNotInitializedYet: VsCode SFDX-Hardis is not initialized yet, please wait a few seconds before running this command again.\nIf the problem persists, update VsCode setting vsCodeSfdxHardis.userInputCommandLineIfLWC to terminal.
waitForCurrentCommand: 🦙 Wait for the current command to be completed before running a new one 😇
websocketServerUnableToStart:
Local WebSocket Server was unable to start.
User prompts will be in the terminal.
welcomeTitle: Welcome to VS Code SFDX Hardis
withFlowHistoryHelp: Include visual diff history for Flows (requires git depth).
workspaceNameHelp:
The folder name for this workspace.
Use PascalCase format (e.g., OpportunityFiles).
This will create a folder in scripts/files/ directory.
workspaceTemplateHelp: Select a predefined template to pre-populate the workspace configuration.
